OpenClaw plus GLM5. Turbo is insane, and I'm about to show you why. There's a brand new AI model that was built just for AI agents. It's fast, it's powerful, and when you pair it with OpenClaw, it can do things that would take you hours in just minutes. The best part, you don't need to be a coder. You don't need a big team. You just need to know how to use it. And that's exactly what I'm going to break down for you right now. So stick around because by the end of this video, you'll know how to set this up and start using it today. All right, so here's the deal. AI agents have had one big problem. Speed. See, an AI agent works in a loop. It looks at something. It thinks about it. It takes an action. It uses a tool. Then it does it all over again, over and over. And if the AI model behind it is slow, the whole thing falls apart. You're just sitting there waiting and waiting. And that's not useful for anyone. That's where GLM5 Turbo comes in. GLM5 is a huge AI model made by a company called GPU AI. We're talking about 744 billion parameters. That's massive. But here's the clever part. It uses something called mixture of experts. So even though the full model is 744 billion parameters, only about 40 billion are active at any one time. That means you get the brain power of a giant model, but it runs way faster. Hey, if we haven't met already, I'm the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of SEO agency Goldie Agency. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. Now, the Turbo version takes that even further. GLM5 Turbo was built from the ground up for AI agents. It's tuned for speed. It's tuned for tool calling. It's tuned for multi-step tasks. So instead of your agent sitting there thinking for 10 seconds between every step, it fires through tasks like nothing. And it can hold up to 200,000 tokens in its context window. That means your agent can remember a ton of information. Past actions, whole documents, code bases, logs. It doesn't forget what it was doing halfway through. Now, let me tell you why this matters for you and your business. Let's say you run a community like we do with the AI profit boardroom and you want to use AI to grow it. You want more content, more engagement, more people finding you online, but you don't have time to do everything yourself. This is where OpenClaw Plus GLM5 Turbo gets really exciting. OpenClaw is an open-source AI agent tool. You can give it a task and it will go do it. It can browse the web, write code, run commands, plan things out. And now with GLM5 Turbo as the brain behind it, it does all of that fast. Let me give you a real example. You could open up OpenClaw and type something like this. Research the top 10 AI automation tools that launched this week and write a blog post for the AI profit boardroom that explains how each tool can help business owners save time and grow faster. And the agent goes to work, it researches, it finds the tools, it summarizes them, it writes the whole blog post all while you go do something else. That's not a dream. That's what this thing does. Here's another one. You could say, "Build a full landing page for the AI profit boardroom that explains the benefits of AI automation for business owners. shows why our community is the best place to learn and it's designed to convert visitors into members. The agent writes the code, it builds the page, it fixes errors, and you've got a working landing page without hiring a developer. To use GLM5 Turbo with OpenClaw, you connect through the ZI provider. You run a simple command like OpenClaw on board and choose the ZI API key option. Then in your config, you set the model to something like ZM5 Turbo. That's it. Now your OpenClaw agent is running on GLM5 Turbo. And this is where it gets fun. Think about all the tasks you do every week for your business. Writing emails, creating social posts, researching competitors, building pages, fixing code. Now, imagine you could hand all of that to an AI agent that just does it fast without you sitting there babysitting it. Let me give you one more example that I think is really powerful. You type into open claw, collect the 10 most viral posts about AI automation on social media this week and turn them into content ideas for the AI profit boardroom YouTube channel and newsletter. The agent goes out, finds the post, reads them, pulls out the key ideas, and gives you a list of readytouse content ideas. That's an hour of work done in minutes. Now, you might be wondering, how does GLM5 Turbo compare to other models? Here's a simple breakdown. GLM5, the full version, is best for deep, complex reasoning. GLM5 Turbo is best for fast agent tasks. Think of it like this. GLM5 is the professor who thinks carefully about every answer. GLM5 Turbo is the assistant who gets things done at lightning speed. For agent work, speed wins, and the benchmarks back this up. GLM5 scores strong in math, coding, and agent tasks. The Turbo version keeps that intelligence, but moves way faster through multi-step workflows. And here's the thing most people miss. It's not just about one task. It's about chaining tasks together. You tell the agent to research something, then write about it, then format it, then publish it. Each step needs the model to think and respond. If every step takes 10 seconds, a 10-step task takes almost 2 minutes of just waiting. With Turbo, those steps fly by. That's the difference between a tool you actually use and a tool that collects dust. The other big deal because GLM5 Turbo only uses about 40 billion active parameters at a time instead of the full 744 billion. Now, here's what I want you to take away from this. AI agents are not a future thing. They're a right now thing, and the models powering them are getting better every single month. GLM5 Turbo is a perfect example. A model with 744 billion parameters tuned specifically for agents running fast enough to be actually useful in the real world.
